title: WIP - podman-remote pull: short-name prompt
body:
Let the Podman remote client properly resolve short names and present
the _same_ prompt as the local client.

This requires vendoring two things:

 * c/common: `libimage` has a new `PullCandidates()` API which returns
   string slice of potential pull candidates.  `PullCandidates()` is
   using the code of `Pull()` and hence is not at risk of letting the
   remote and the local client diverge over time.

 * c/image: `pkg/shortnames` has new `Prompt()` API which is opening the
   exact same prompt as the local client (again code share).

With the upper backend plumbing in place, Podman now exposes a new REST
endpoint `images/$name/resolve` which returns slice of strings from the
server's `PullCandidates()` which are then presented in the remote
client via `pkg/shortnames.Prompt()`.

During a `pull foo`, the remote client first calls `images/foo/resolve`
and then calls `Prompt()` on the results.  Note that `Prompt()` may
return `foo` instead of, for instance, foo's alias.  The reasoning
behind is that subsequently pulling foo will preserve the same log
messages as the local client (e.g., "Resolve $image as an alias ...").

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@jwhonce, can you check if the new images/$name/resolve endpoint is sufficient REST-ish? It's calling GET on an object that may not exist, but I am not sure whether that's OK or not>

@mtrmac, that's bringing containers/image#1384 and https://github.com/containers/common/pull/798 in. I have absolutely no clue what to do with remote build or remote play kube yet but I the plumbing for prompting is done.

@vrothberg: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
Closing as we don't pursue remote prompting anymore but solve it by configuring `podman machine` VMs.
